action: Recover and formalize the distinction between canonical shared queue state and additive team-owned queue projections.
selected_because: Recent team runs committed queue-update records while operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json remained unchanged. Without a durable state-layer distinction, later consumers could either erase valid progress or overstate projections as canonical and verified.

observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is the named canonical shared queue baseline
recent teams committed additive queue-update records without replacing ACTIVE_QUEUE.json
inferred: canonical queue state and additive projections are separate evidence layers
proposed: use a deterministic read-only reducer before any canonical consolidation
unresolved: whether any existing reducer or workflow consolidates projections
which projection should next be canonically adopted

verification: GitHub accepted the append-only continuity artifact and returned commit b94f90e27e1d896622a69de0c75bcee92c432e02. The cited canonical blobs and recent queue-update commits were inspected. No canonical consolidation, runtime result, deployment, or peer execution is claimed.
privacy_boundary: Only public repository files, public commit metadata, and abstracted non-identifying coordination language were used. Raw private chat, health and pet-health material, credentials, identifying data, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
handoff: Frontier Research should specify reducer invariants and adversarial tests; Vercel Studio and Cloudflare Research should identify which queue fields require independently bound runtime or deployment evidence; Independent Creative Web should preserve its identity boundary during consolidation.
